summary: Loading-placeholder atom with text, circular, and rectangular shapes mapped from the synced Figma Skeleton page into one core recipe and shared React/Vue adapter contract.
philosophy: Keep Skeleton as a low-level loading placeholder atom rather than a layout-specific card or list skeleton family.
Keep the three synced baseline shapes source-owned in core so React and Vue render the same dimensions, radius rules, metadata, and accessibility behavior.
Keep visual treatment in preset CSS through stable mw-skeleton classes and theme-relative variables instead of adapter-owned styling.
Keep the atom decorative by default, with explicit ariaLabel opt-in for standalone status usage.

useWhen: The UI needs a lightweight placeholder for loading text, avatar/media circles, or rectangular content blocks.
The product already owns the surrounding loading context and the skeleton can stay decorative.
React and Vue consumers should receive the same placeholder dimensions, shape variants, animation choices, and accessibility contract.
avoidWhen: The experience needs determinate progress, percentage tracking, or an indeterminate activity indicator instead of structural placeholders.
The implementation cannot provide a truthful ariaLabel when a standalone skeleton region must announce loading status.
The product needs a layout-specific skeleton composition that should live as an adapter/story composition rather than core atom API.

automationCovers: Core recipe tests for decorative defaults, Figma baseline dimensions, custom dimensions, animation modes, and standalone status mode.
Preset CSS contract tests for the Figma 4% text overlay mapping plus pulse and wave animation hooks.
Shared React/Vue adapter contract tests for DOM metadata, accessibility output, dimensions, and animation classes.
Storybook introduction/taxonomy tests and a11y smoke coverage in both React and Vue Storybook apps.
manualReviewRequired: Product-level decisions about when a skeleton is decorative versus when a loading region needs an announced status label.
Visual review against the synced Figma page after V3 Skeleton design-system sources become more complete.
notes: The current Figma source is V2/MUI-derived and only provides baseline shapes, so this family intentionally ships a small atom API first.

openQuestions: Should V3 introduce named layout skeleton compositions after the design source graduates from the V2/MUI-derived baseline page?
